title: Dense Ethiopian Speck Ye-beg-alicha
description: This recipe brings together tender speck, herbal notes, and a tangy ferment-kimchi. Serve with injera or Ethiopian flatbread for a truly immersive experience.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook the speck until browned, about 4 minutes per side.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the ferment-kimchi, oregano, cumin, paprika, and salt to the skillet and stir to combine.
Stir in the injera flour and cook for 1 minute.
Using a spatula, shape the mixture into a round shape.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, scraping the bottom of the pan with a spatula to prevent sticking.
Serve warm with injera or Ethiopian flatbread.
Let the ye-beg-alicha rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
Slice the ye-beg-alicha into wedges and serve with your choice of sides.
